name: Peak of the Snows – trail run from The Bridge
name_original: Pico de las Nieves – trail run desde El Puente
region_intro: Jogging routes around Guriezo offer a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by a unique blend of coastal proximity and imposing rocky mountains. The region features lush meadows, green forests, and woodlands, providing picturesque views and varied terrain. Guriezo's natural environment includes significant peaks like Cerredo mountain and Pico de las Nieves, alongside karstic formations and ravines, making it suitable for a range of running experiences. Best jogging routes around Guriezo The most popular jogging route is Peak of the Snows – trail run from The Bridge, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 53 minutes to complete, featuring a challenging ascent towards Pico de las Nieves. Another top favourite among local runners is Devil's Eye loop from Oriñón, a difficult 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This route offers dramatic coastal scenery and unique rock formations known as the "Ojos del Demonio." Local runners also love the Devil's Eye loop from Cueto Gedo, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail leading through coastal cliffs and viewpoints, often completed in about 54 minutes. Jogging around Guriezo is defined by imposing mountains, lush woodlands, and coastal paths with panoramic views. The extensive network of running routes offers options for various fitness levels, from easy paths to more demanding trails with significant elevation gains. The routes in Guriezo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 2,500 runners have used komoot to explore Guriezo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Guriezo?
A: Guriezo offers a wide selection of over 130 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These trails traverse diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents, ensuring there's something for every runner.

Q: Are there easy running paths suitable for beginners in Guriezo?
A: Yes, Guriezo has several easy running paths perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed jog. There are 7 routes specifically categorized as easy, offering gentle terrain and beautiful scenery without significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24661584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenillas Beach – Views of El Pendo loop from Playa de Arenillas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that offers pleasant coastal views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Guriezo?
A: Jogging trails in Guriezo offer a highly varied terrain. You can expect a unique blend of coastal paths with panoramic sea views, lush meadows, green forests, and challenging mountainous sections. The region features significant peaks like Cerredo mountain and Pico de las Nieves, alongside karstic formations and ravines, providing diverse running experiences.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Guriezo?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Guriezo are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. This is convenient for exploring the area without needing to arrange transport back. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31859907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de las Nieves loop from Guriezo&lt;/a&gt; or the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10509453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye loop from Oriñón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the running routes?
A: Guriezo's running routes are rich with natural beauty and interesting landmarks. You can encounter dramatic coastal scenery, unique rock formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1834895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye&lt;/a&gt;, and impressive mountain peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4486690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solpico Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4072442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Snows&lt;/a&gt; at the summit of Pico de las Nieves also offers a cultural element to your run.

Q: Are there any running routes with good viewpoints in Guriezo?
A: Absolutely! Guriezo is known for its stunning panoramic views. Trails around Candina mountain offer dramatic coastal vistas over the Liendo Valley, Santoña, and Laredo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1444823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Mina&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent viewpoint, and the summit of Cerredo mountain provides unparalleled views of the Cantabrian Sea shore.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Guriezo?
A: The running experience in Guriezo is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain trails to picturesque coastal paths, and the stunning panoramic views. The variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging routes in Guriezo?
A: While many routes in Guriezo involve significant elevation, there are easier paths suitable for families looking for a gentle jog or walk. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' or 'moderate' with shorter distances and less ascent. The coastal areas, particularly around Oriñon beach during low tide, can also offer flatter, more accessible options for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Guriezo?
A: Many natural trails in Guriezo are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy a run with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual trails. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Guriezo?
A: Guriezo offers good jogging conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for exploring both coastal and mountain trails. Summer can be warm, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended. Winter offers a different charm, with cooler temperatures and potentially snow on higher peaks, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any running routes that are approximately 5km long?
A: Yes, Guriezo offers several routes around the 5km mark. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14997422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye loop from Cueto Gedo&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.3-mile (5.3 km) trail that provides coastal cliffs and viewpoints, typically completed in under an hour. These shorter routes are great for a quick workout or a scenic jog.

Q: Is parking available near the jogging trails in Guriezo?
A: Parking availability varies depending on the specific trailhead. Many villages and popular starting points for trails, such as those leading to Pico de las Nieves or coastal areas, often have designated parking spots or roadside parking. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route for specific parking information before you go.
tips: The top of Pico de las Nieves is located at 774 meters above sea level and offers beautiful views of the Cantabrian interior. Right here is the hermitage that venerates Guriezano every August 5.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of the Snows
